summary: Decompiled and typed more of src/melee/gm/gmmain_lib, primarily around name tag and persistent stats initialization/reset paths. The PR added full matches for gmMainLib_8015F260, InitializePersistentNameData, .sdata2, and finished gmMainLib_8015FA34, while substantially improving partial matches for gmMainLib_8015DBF4, gmMainLib_8015F600, gmMainLib_8015EA80, and gmMainLib_8015F150. Header prototypes were tightened from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete s32/void signatures.

key_files.role: Implemented previously stubbed/partial gmmain_lib routines and adjusted matching details.
Updated prototypes for newly understood functions and argument/return types.

what_changed: Added includes needed by the new decompilation work: placeholder.h, melee/if/textlib.h, melee/mn/mnname.h, and melee/ty/tylist.h.
Implemented gmMainLib_8015DBF4 as a partial match that adjusts stored name tag/player indices across gmMainLib_804D3EE0, gm_80497618, many VsModeData-like fields, and selected GameRules fields.
Implemented gmMainLib_8015EA80 to reset selected player/name-tag data and set handicap values to 9 across repeated VsModeData player arrays.
Added InitializePersistentNameData(s32), which clears a NameTagData record's vs_kos, sd_count-style stats, play_time_by_fighter, and sets x1A2 to 5.
Added gmMainLib_8015F150 to clear fighter_kos and sd_count-like stats for 25 FighterData entries.
Added gmMainLib_8015F260 to initialize 120 NameTagData entries using bank/index arithmetic over x2FF8.
Implemented most of gmMainLib_8015F600, including full-save reset behavior for arg0 == 1 and name-tag-bank reset/copy behavior otherwise.
Introduced static u8 gmMainLib_804D3EE4, producing the bot-reported .sdata2 match.

Finished gmMainLib_8015FA34 by adding PAD_STACK(50), #pragma inline_depth(3), changing its parameter type to s32, assigning x186C as 0xFF instead of -1, and removing the prior inline-related todo comment.
Updated gmmain_lib.h signatures: gmMainLib_8015DBF4 now returns s32, gmMainLib_8015EA80/gmMainLib_8015F150/gmMainLib_8015F260 are void(void), and gmMainLib_8015FA34 takes s32.
smart_moves: Used small local macros ADJ_NAMETAG_78 and ADJ_VMD inside gmMainLib_8015DBF4 to express a repeated index-adjustment pattern without duplicating all compare/decrement logic.
Preserved byte-sized behavior explicitly with u8 casts around arg0 and indices, which is important for matching code that truncates name tag/player indices.
Kept repeated reset loops structured around original constants such as 6, 19, 25, and 120, matching the apparent storage layout for players, name-tag banks, fighters, and matchup stats.
Used bank/index decomposition, e.g. index / 19 and index % 19, to access NameTagDataBank entries in the same layout as the underlying data.
Applied PAD_STACK and #pragma inline_depth around functions where stack shape or inlining affected the match.
Converted x186C assignment from -1 to 0xFF, making the intended byte value explicit and helping finish gmMainLib_8015FA34.
decomp_lessons: When a function manipulates packed save/name-tag records, concrete unsigned-byte casts can be necessary even when the source-level logic looks like normal signed integer arithmetic.
For repeated struct-field adjustments across many similar embedded structs, local macros can make a partial decomp readable while preserving the exact repeated code shape reviewers can inspect.
Header cleanup should follow decomp progress: replacing UNK_RET and UNK_PARAMS with concrete prototypes documents newly learned ABI facts and improves call-site type checking.
Persistent-data reset routines often reveal struct layout through repeated zeroing loops and fixed-size arrays; use those constants to refine names and field grouping conservatively.
A small data symbol such as static u8 gmMainLib_804D3EE4 can be enough to recover a data-section match when it represents a sentinel value used by string/name copying.
Do not over-name still-unknown layout fields: this PR retains fields like unk_590, x2FF8, x1F2C, and x1A2 where evidence is not strong enough for semantic names.
naming_conventions: Matched project convention for address-based unknown functions: gmMainLib_8015DBF4, gmMainLib_8015EA80, gmMainLib_8015F150, gmMainLib_8015F260, gmMainLib_8015F600, gmMainLib_8015FA34.
Used a semantic name where intent was clearer: InitializePersistentNameData(s32).
Kept struct/type names already present or inferred elsewhere, including GameRules, VsModeData, NameTagData, NameTagDataBank, FighterData, and struct gmMainLib_8015EF30_s.
Maintained unknown-field naming style for unresolved fields: unk_51C, unk_522, unk_528, unk_530, unk_584, unk_586, unk_1490, x2FF8, x1F2C, x1A2, x1CD0, x1CB0.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted PAD_STACK(16), PAD_STACK(8), and PAD_STACK(50) in functions where stack padding was needed for matching.
Used #pragma push, #pragma inline_depth(3), and #pragma pop around gmMainLib_8015FA34 to control inlining depth for the final match.
Rewrote gmMainLib_8015FA34 parameter from int to s32 and local loop variables to s32 to align with project/platform type expectations.
Used explicit u8 casts for indices and comparisons in name-tag and bank access paths.
Used do-while loops in gmMainLib_8015F600 for reset loops, likely reflecting the original control-flow shape better than for-loops.
Declared gmMainLib_804D3EE4 as static u8 to match the newly reported .sdata2 item.
Changed a byte-field assignment from -1 to 0xFF, avoiding signedness/codegen mismatch.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The decomp-dev bot reported matched code improved to 63.83% (+0.03%, +1284 bytes) and matched data to 38.25% (+8 bytes).
Bot-reported new 100% matches: gmMainLib_8015F260 (+516 bytes), InitializePersistentNameData (+460 bytes), .sdata2 (+8 bytes), and gmMainLib_8015FA34 finishing from 98.30% to 100.00%.

Bot-reported partial improvements: gmMainLib_8015DBF4 to 59.65% (+2221 bytes), gmMainLib_8015F600 to 86.01% (+925 bytes), gmMainLib_8015EA80 to 89.38% (+500 bytes), and gmMainLib_8015F150 to 76.97% (+209 bytes).
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish gmMainLib_8015DBF4 from 59.65% to 100%.
Find later PRs that finish gmMainLib_8015F600 from 86.01% to 100%.
Search for struct cleanup around NameTagDataBank, FighterData, and gmMainLib_804D3EE0->thing fields x2FF8, x1F2C, x1CD0, and x1CB0.
Search for uses or renames of gmMainLib_804D3EE4 to identify the sentinel/string terminator semantics.
Compare later name-tag reset implementations for whether ADJ_NAMETAG_78 and ADJ_VMD were retained or replaced by clearer helper functions.
